steps.title: Gather the source material
steps.detail: Start with the real inputs for writing social media captions from post ideas: notes, files, transcript text, product facts, or examples. The output will only be useful if the source is specific.

steps.title: Ask for the first useful draft
steps.detail: Use Ocoya to create the first pass from that source material. Ask for a practical result, not a generic explanation of the topic.

steps.title: Check the draft against the original
steps.detail: Compare names, facts, dates, claims, missing context, and tone against the original input before you edit style or formatting.

steps.title: Change format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use Surfer SEO when the result needs a visual, social, presentation, or alternate format. Keep the same source facts so the rewrite does not drift.

steps.title: Polish the final version
steps.detail: Use ElevenLabs or a manual review pass to tighten wording, remove filler, and make the final result easier to read on a phone or computer.

faqs.question: Can I use AI to turn writing social media captions from post ideas into something usable without making it generic?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you start with a clear angle and edit the result for your audience. Ocoya is the best starting point for the first draft, Surfer SEO is useful when you need a different format or angle, and ElevenLabs helps when the copy needs a final cleanup.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better for repurposing content?
faqs.answer: Ocoya usually works best for the first rewrite, while Surfer SEO is better when the job shifts toward a different style or asset format.

faqs.question: Is Ocoya better than Surfer SEO for this workflow?
faqs.answer: Ocoya is better when you want a direct content pass; Surfer SEO is better when the same input needs a new angle, layout, or structure.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I publish or post it?
faqs.answer: Avoid posting the first output without checking the hook, the flow, and the factual details. Use ElevenLabs as the last cleanup step, not the only step.
